description: C01 Buyer would switch from incumbent. Pursue stage, bpo_managed_service, role=challenger. The team's entire switching case rests on the fact that the buyer has issued a competitive tender. No specific performance weakness of the incumbent is named, no documented buyer dissatisfaction is referenced, and the evidence explicitly relies on 'we think they want change because they issued a tender' — which the C01 content identifies as the textbook insufficient switching rationale. Expected: Challenged — a competitive tender is not evidence of switching appetite. Tests that Alex enforces the C01 standard that a running competition does not imply buyer intent to displace the incumbent.

context.notes: ITT published 3 weeks ago. Incumbent has held the contract for 10 years. No pre-market engagement was held. Team has not spoken directly to any key stakeholders prior to ITT publication.

evidence: We think the buyer wants change because they have published a fully open competitive tender rather than a direct award or restricted process. This signals to us that the Welsh Government is open to switching from the current provider and has not decided to simply renew. The fact that they are running a competition means they want to consider alternatives, which is why we believe there is a real switching appetite here. We are confident that if we put forward a strong proposition the buyer will seriously consider moving away from the incumbent.
idealResponseFromWorkbook: Hand-crafted: C01 benchmarkExamples (SD) explicitly states 'We think the buyer may switch because they are running a competition. No specific, documented switching case is evidenced.' This fixture directly exercises that benchmark. A competitive tender is a procurement requirement, not a switching signal. Alex should challenge and downgrade to Strongly Disagree.
